title: buildah: add `heredoc` support for `RUN`, `COPY` and `ADD`
body:
Following PR is a attempt to add `Heredoc` support to buildah.

Once this PR is merged buildah is supposed to honor heredoc syntax while processing containerfiles

Expected syntax to work

```Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/library/python:latest
RUN <<EOF
echo "Hello" >> /hello
echo "World!" >> /hello
EOF

RUN python3 <<EOF
with open("/hello", "w") as f:
    print("Hello", file=f)
    print("Something", file=f)
EOF

RUN ls -a
RUN cat hello
```

Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

This needs: https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/264

Other then the imagebuilder PR, following PR is ready to review.

@nalind @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg @giuseppe @containers/buildah-maintainers PTAL

/hold wait till https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/264

Changes here LGTM in general.  I assume you'll also be updating [the Containerfile man page in](https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/docs/Containerfile.5.md) in c/common with the heredocs examples?

> Changes here LGTM in general. I assume you'll also be updating [the Containerfile man page in](https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/docs/Containerfile.5.md) in c/common with the heredocs examples?

@TomSweeneyRedHat Yes I'll open a PR in `c/common` once this gets merged. As of now I am waiting for https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/264
This PR is read for review.

I just took a quick looking at this after the demo, why are we importing all these protobuf files from builtkit? I understand that reuse is good but is there a way to do that without this useless protobuf code?

I am worried because a) that adds about 800k binary size and b) because it uses init() function it slows down every single command and allocates more memory, you check it by running buildah with `GODEBUG=inittrace=1`:
```
...
init github.com/gogo/protobuf/types @13 ms, 0.064 ms clock, 13264 bytes, 86 allocs
init github.com/containerd/typeurl @13 ms, 0.010 ms clock, 352 bytes, 4 allocs
init github.com/moby/buildkit/util/stack @13 ms, 0.13 ms clock, 87312 bytes, 58 allocs
init github.com/moby/buildkit/frontend/dockerfile/command @13 ms, 0.002 ms clock, 624 bytes, 2 allocs
init github.com/moby/buildkit/frontend/dockerfile/parser @13 ms, 0.079 ms clock, 23392 bytes, 262 allocs
...
```

To be clear I am not saying this is a blocker but we certainly should look into if we can prevent this from getting pulled in because IMO it should not slow down every single buildah/podman command as it is generally not needed. But I guess this is a lost battle within the go ecosystem.

@Luap99 I'm not sure if we can change this function like this, cause buildkit is using this so this patch will not be accepted at buildkit's end. Will it work if we move this to some other package different than `parser` and then buildkit uses it ? (That can still be accepted but will it reduce the protobuf dep ? )

> This needs conformance tests, since I'm concerned about whether or not we're adding (or not) newlines at end-of-file for COPY in a way that's consistent with expectations.

Conformance infra or `c/image` is unable to parse docker reference generated by docker daemon when a file with heredock is provided causing conformance test unable to process any heredoc files. I think `c/image`'s `docker daemon's` ParseReference needs to be fixed so it can read new buildkit images correctly. I am working on fixing `c/image` however I am manually verified output of `COPY` and `ADD`.

Sample containerfile which conformance test cannot read
```Dockerfile
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.4
FROM alpine
COPY <<robots.txt <<humans.txt /test/
(robots content)
robots.txt
(humans content)
humans.txt
```

> @Luap99 I'm not sure if we can change this function like this, cause buildkit is using this so this patch will not be accepted at buildkit's end. Will it work if we move this to some other package different than `parser` and then buildkit uses it ? (That can still be accepted but will it reduce the protobuf dep ? )

Yeah that is what I am assuming too, the issue is that as soon `github.com/moby/buildkit/util/stack` is important you end up with the the protobuf packages as well which add ton of bloat.
I am not sure if buildkit upstream would care about something like that anyway, The easiest solution would be to have a special build tag for example or I guess we could fork it.
Either way I not saying we should block this but there i definitely room for improvements.
This PR needs a rebase after https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5165 since it uses a commit from the same PR.
Grats on getting this in @flouthoc !  Nice work again!
